name: ExtensionActorDefinition_UseContext
title: R5: The context that the content is intended to support (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `ActorDefinition.useContext` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`ActorDefinition.useContext` 0..* `UsageContext`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
* R5 `ActorDefinition.useContext` 0..* `UsageContext`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `ActorDefinition.useContext` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ActorDefinition` is not mapped.
Element `ActorDefinition.useContext` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: The context that the content is intended to support (new)
element.comment:
Element `ActorDefinition.useContext` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ActorDefinition` is not mapped.
Element `ActorDefinition.useContext` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
When multiple useContexts are specified, there is no expectation that all or any of the contexts apply.

element.short: The context that the content is intended to support
element.definition: The content was developed with a focus and intent of supporting the contexts that are listed. These contexts may be general categories (gender, age, ...) or may be references to specific programs (insurance plans, studies, ...) and may be used to assist with indexing and searching for appropriate actor definition instances.
element.comment: When multiple useContexts are specified, there is no expectation that all or any of the contexts apply.
